description:
Pre-existing flaky test surfaced after mutagen-hcs.9 fixed the ast spec verification grep (2026-06-24). It keeps `mix spec.check` intermittently red (mutagen.ast's `mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs` verification command) AND can intermittently fail the new GitHub Actions CI (.5 runs `mix test` under a random seed).

## Bug
test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs, test "does NOT call String.to_atom on the target string (r8 invariant)" (~line 135) snapshots the GLOBAL atom table:
    before_count = :erlang.system_info(:atom_count)
    assert Ast.find_module_body(ast, target) == :not_found
    after_count = :erlang.system_info(:atom_count)
    assert before_count == after_count
ast_test runs async with max_cases:20, so a CONCURRENT async test minting an atom between the two snapshots makes after_count > before_count and the assertion fails (memory note: 37994 vs 37996). Passes in isolation, flakes in the full suite / under some seeds.

## Fix (scope: test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs, and only if needed test_helper)
Make the r8 atom-count invariant non-racy WITHOUT weakening what it checks (the real invariant: Ast.find_module_body must not call String.to_atom on the target). Preferred approaches, pick the soundest:
- Make this specific test (or a dedicated describe block / the module) `async: false` so no other test runs concurrently during the atom-count window — simplest, keeps the global-counter check honest.
- OR assert the invariant more robustly: e.g. call find_module_body many times in a tight loop and assert the atom count does not grow by N (a concurrent one-off atom mint is dwarfed), or check that the specific target atom was NOT created (`:erlang...`/catch ArgumentError on `String.to_existing_atom(target)` after the call) rather than a global delta.
Keep the test meaningful: it must still FAIL if find_module_body ever calls String.to_atom on the target.

## Acceptance
- `mix spec.check` => status=pass errors=0 (mutagen.ast green; run it a few times to confirm no flake).
- The test still catches a regression: temporarily make find_module_body call String.to_atom(target) and confirm the test fails; revert.
- `mix test` green across several seeds (e.g. seeds 0, 1, 42, plus a couple random) AND `make smoke` green repeated runs.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED (commit 901e928)

Deflaked the r8 atom-count invariant in test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs (test 'does NOT call String.to_atom on the target string', line 135).

Root cause: the test snapshotted the GLOBAL :erlang.system_info(:atom_count) before/after find_module_body/2 and asserted no delta. The file is async (max_cases:20), so a concurrent test minting an atom in that window failed the global-delta assertion (e.g. 37994 vs 37996). Passed in isolation, flaked in the full suite.

Fix (chosen: precise per-target assertion, not async:false): assert the specific target atom does NOT exist before the call AND still does NOT exist after — String.to_existing_atom(target) must raise ArgumentError both times. If find_module_body ever called String.to_atom(target), the atom would intern and the post-call assert_raise would fail. This depends only on the target atom, never the global counter, so concurrent atom minting cannot affect it. The invariant is also now stated more precisely than the old global-delta proxy.

Acceptance:
- mix spec.check: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 on 3 consecutive runs. (The 'Mix.raise: 44 branch finding(s)' nonzero exit is pre-existing on the pristine base dcf06ba — identical 44 count and same status=pass errors=0 with my change stashed — it is the branch-coverage check against base mutagen-hcs/hex-pm-publish-hygiene, unrelated to this deflake.)
- Regression-proof: temporarily added '_ = String.to_atom(target_mod_str)' to find_module_body -> test FAILED ('Expected exception ArgumentError but nothing was raised' at ast_test.exs:153, 1 failure). Reverted; lib/ is clean.
- mix test green across seeds 0, 1, 42, 7, 1234 (643 tests, 0 failures each).
- make smoke green on 2 repeated runs.

Scope: test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs only. lib/ product code and the spec are unchanged. .spec/state.json was NOT committed: a direct 'mix spec.check' invocation regenerated it and dropped the realization block (off-nominal vs the verification harness), so I restored it to base to avoid regressing mutation/realization tracking.

APPROVED (orchestrator). Flake fixed precisely: the racy global :atom_count delta is replaced with a per-target `assert_raise ArgumentError, fn -> String.to_existing_atom(target) end` before AND after the call — concurrency-proof and a sharper statement of the r8 invariant. Independently verified: mix test green across seeds 0/13/99 (643/0 each); worker regression-proved it (adding String.to_atom(target) -> test fails). spec.check VALIDATION is now errors=0 (staleness + flake gone). Scope: test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs only. Merging.

Residual spec.check nonzero exit is NOT staleness/flake and does NOT block: 5 branch_guard_missing_subject_update (branch-relative vs origin/main — vanish on merge) + ~44 overlap/duplicate_covers (pre-existing on main from the SpecLedEx corpus). Tracking decision deferred to user.
Merged --ff-only into epic. Gates green: mix test --seed 0 643/0 (+ seeds 13/99 green, no flake), format clean, deps.unlock clean. Closing.

title: Deflake ast_test atom-count assertion (racy global :atom_count under async)
